userStories.title: FTS Optimize Strategy Interface & Implementations
userStories.description: Create the FtsOptimizeStrategy interface and three concrete implementations: CounterOptimizeStrategy (optimize after N inserts per project), CronOptimizeStrategy (optimize dirty tables on a configurable interval via SchedulerRegistry), and ManualOptimizeStrategy (no-op on insert, lazy startup optimize only). All strategies must be @Injectable() NestJS classes using Logger from @nestjs/common. The interface exports the FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY injection token. Unit tests cover all three strategies with mocked LanceDB table.optimize().
acceptanceCriteria: FtsOptimizeStrategy interface exports the string token FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY from fts-optimize-strategy.interface.ts
CounterOptimizeStrategy.onInsert() calls table.optimize() when the insert count for projectId reaches the configured threshold
CounterOptimizeStrategy.onInsert() does NOT call table.optimize() when the insert count is below the threshold
CounterOptimizeStrategy.onInsert() resets the counter for projectId to 0 after calling table.optimize()
CounterOptimizeStrategy.onInsert() logs a warning and does not throw when table.optimize() rejects
CounterOptimizeStrategy.onFirstAccess() calls table.optimize() without awaiting (fire-and-forget) for the given projectId
CounterOptimizeStrategy reads threshold from ConfigService at key 'rag.ftsOptimizeThreshold', defaulting to 10 when not set
CronOptimizeStrategy.onInsert() adds the table reference to the internal dirtyTables map for the given projectId
CronOptimizeStrategy.optimizeDirtyTables() calls table.optimize() for each entry in dirtyTables and clears the map afterward
CronOptimizeStrategy registers an interval via SchedulerRegistry.addInterval('fts-optimize', interval) during construction
CronOptimizeStrategy reads interval from ConfigService at key 'rag.ftsOptimizeIntervalMs', defaulting to 300000 when not set
CronOptimizeStrategy.onDestroy() flushes all remaining dirty tables by calling optimizeDirtyTables()
CronOptimizeStrategy.onFirstAccess() calls table.optimize() without awaiting (fire-and-forget) for the given projectId
ManualOptimizeStrategy.onInsert() does not call table.optimize() regardless of call count
ManualOptimizeStrategy.onFirstAccess() calls table.optimize() without awaiting (fire-and-forget) for the given projectId
ManualOptimizeStrategy.onDestroy() completes without error

userStories.title: Config & Module Wiring for FTS Optimize Strategy
userStories.description: Add three FTS optimize config keys to rag.config.ts (ftsOptimizeStrategy, ftsOptimizeThreshold, ftsOptimizeIntervalMs). Update RagModule to import ScheduleModule.forRoot() and register a factory provider that resolves FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY to the correct strategy instance based on the config value. Install @nestjs/schedule as a dependency in apps/api/package.json.
acceptanceCriteria: rag.config.ts exposes ftsOptimizeStrategy defaulting to 'counter' when FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY env var is not set
rag.config.ts exposes ftsOptimizeThreshold defaulting to 10 when FTS_OPTIMIZE_THRESHOLD env var is not set
rag.config.ts exposes ftsOptimizeIntervalMs defaulting to 300000 when FTS_OPTIMIZE_INTERVAL_MS env var is not set
RagModule provides ScheduleModule.forRoot() in its imports array
When rag.ftsOptimizeStrategy config is 'cron', the FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY provider resolves to the CronOptimizeStrategy instance
When rag.ftsOptimizeStrategy config is 'manual', the FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY provider resolves to the ManualOptimizeStrategy instance
When rag.ftsOptimizeStrategy config is 'counter' or any unknown value, the FTS_OPTIMIZE_STRATEGY provider resolves to the CounterOptimizeStrategy instance
apps/api/package.json lists @nestjs/schedule as a production dependency

userStories.title: Migrate RagService to Native Tantivy FTS
userStories.description: Replace the in-memory FTS scan in RagService.search() with LanceDB native Tantivy FTS using table.search(query, 'fts', 'content'). Add FTS index creation via Index.fts() in getOrCreateTable(). Inject FtsOptimizeStrategy and call onInsert() after every indexDocument(), onFirstAccess() on first table access per session, and onDestroy() in onModuleDestroy(). Keep simpleFtsScore and the in-memory FTS path as fallback for InMemoryTable and when native FTS throws.
acceptanceCriteria: When lanceAvailable is true and table.search() resolves, search() uses native FTS results ranked by reciprocal position score (1 / (i + 1))
When lanceAvailable is true and table.search() rejects, search() logs a warning and falls back to in-memory FTS using simpleFtsScore
When lanceAvailable is false (inMemoryOnly mode), search() uses in-memory FTS using simpleFtsScore without calling table.search()
getOrCreateTable() calls table.createIndex('content', { config: Index.fts(...), replace: false }) when lanceAvailable is true
getOrCreateTable() does not call table.createIndex() when lanceAvailable is false
getOrCreateTable() logs a warning and does not throw when createIndex() rejects
getOrCreateTable() calls optimizeStrategy.onFirstAccess(projectId, table) exactly once per projectId per service instance lifetime when lanceAvailable is true
indexDocument() calls optimizeStrategy.onInsert(projectId, table) after table.add() when lanceAvailable is true
indexDocument() does not call optimizeStrategy.onInsert() when lanceAvailable is false
onModuleDestroy() calls optimizeStrategy.onDestroy()
simpleFtsScore function remains exported from rag.service.ts
reciprocalRankFusion function remains exported from rag.service.ts
ftsRows returned by native FTS are added to the recordMap used for result resolution

userStories.title: Manual Optimize REST Endpoint
userStories.description: Add POST /api/projects/:slug/kb/optimize endpoint to RagController that triggers table.optimize() for the project's LanceDB table. The endpoint requires ADMIN role and returns { optimized: true } wrapped in JsonResponse.Ok(). Add the corresponding public optimizeTable(projectId) method to RagService. The endpoint is Swagger-documented and follows existing controller patterns (resolveProject helper, ForbiddenAppException).
acceptanceCriteria: RagService.optimizeTable(projectId) calls table.optimize() on the project's LanceDB table when lanceAvailable is true
RagService.optimizeTable(projectId) does not call table.optimize() when lanceAvailable is false
When req.user.extra.role is 'ADMIN', POST /projects/:slug/kb/optimize returns HTTP 200 with body { ret: 0, data: { optimized: true } }
When req.user.extra.role is not 'ADMIN', POST /projects/:slug/kb/optimize returns HTTP 403
When the slug does not match any project, POST /projects/:slug/kb/optimize returns HTTP 404
POST /projects/:slug/kb/optimize has @ApiOperation and @ApiResponse decorators for 200, 403, and 404 status codes

analysis: The existing RAG module lives in apps/api/src/rag/ with these key files: rag.service.ts (hybrid search with in-memory FTS capped at 500 docs), rag.module.ts (standard NestJS module, no ScheduleModule), rag.controller.ts (4 endpoints, no optimize endpoint), rag.service.spec.ts (unit tests for simpleFtsScore, reciprocalRankFusion, getSimilarityTier, getVerdict, onModuleDestroy), and rag.config.ts (registerAs('rag', ...) with inMemoryOnly, ftsIndexMode, thresholds). Key risks: (1) rag.service.ts uses a lanceAvailable guard — the FTS index creation and native search must stay inside that guard, never called on InMemoryTable. (2) simpleFtsScore and reciprocalRankFusion must NOT be removed — they are exported and tested, and simpleFtsScore is used in the in-memory fallback path. (3) CronOptimizeStrategy uses SchedulerRegistry.addInterval() not @Interval() decorator — @nestjs/schedule must be added as a dependency. (4) The factory provider instantiates all three strategies eagerly; SchedulerRegistry must be provided when CronOptimizeStrategy is constructed. (5) createIndex with replace:false is safe to call on every startup — no-op if index exists. (6) Native FTS search uses table.search(query, 'fts', 'content') not table.query(). (7) RagController currently has a resolveProject() helper and uses JsonResponse.Ok() — the optimize endpoint must follow the same pattern. (8) rag.service.spec.ts mocks LanceDB table operations — new tests must follow the same mock pattern. Dependency order is strict: US-001 (strategy files) → US-002 (config + module) → US-003 (RagService migration) → US-004 (controller endpoint).
